name: Chang'e 2
fact: Began as a higher-resolution remap of the Moon following Chang'e 1, then repurposed for an extended deep-space mission. After completing lunar mapping in 2011, Chang'e 2 departed for Sun-Earth L2 — China's first deep-space mission — and then on to a 2012 flyby of asteroid 4179 Toutatis. Still tracked in heliocentric orbit.
capability: First Chinese deep-space mission; first non-NASA-non-ESA mission to L2; first non-Western asteroid flyby.
